query: SpaceX IPO S-1 filing shares outstanding total share count all classes
answer:
This is an excellent and comprehensive result. The agent found detailed data from previously gathered research files. Let me compile the final synthesis.

---

# SpaceX IPO: S-1 Filing, Shares Outstanding, and Share Structure

## S-1 Filing Status

SpaceX (Space Exploration Technologies Corp., CIK: 0001181412) has **filed a public S-1 registration statement** with the SEC:

- **March 30, 2026** — Confidential Draft Registration Statement (DRS) filed
- **May 20, 2026** — Public S-1 registration statement filed (SEC Accession Number: 0001628280-26-036936)
- **Target listing date:** June 12, 2026 on **Nasdaq and Nasdaq Texas** under ticker **"SPCX"**
- Underwriters include Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, BofA Securities, Citigroup, J.P. Morgan, and others

---

## Total Share Count (All Classes)

**Pro forma total shares outstanding: ~12.5 billion shares (12,521 million)** as of March 31, 2026.

This reflects a **5-for-1 stock split** effective May 4, 2026 (pre-split: ~2.5 billion shares).

Additionally, Elon Musk holds a **1 billion restricted share award** contingent on two milestones: (1) a $7.5 trillion market cap, and (2) a permanent Mars colony with 1 million inhabitants. These shares are **not** included in current outstanding counts.

---

## Share Class Structure (Dual-Class)

| Class | Voting Rights | Holders | Status |
|-------|--------------|---------|--------|
| **Class A Common Stock** | 1 vote per share | Public investors (IPO offering) | To be publicly traded |
| **Class B Common Stock** | 10 votes per share | Elon Musk and insiders | Privately retained |

- **Elon Musk controls ~85% of total shareholder voting power** through Class B super-voting shares
- SpaceX will be classified as a **"controlled company"** under Nasdaq listing rules
- Musk will simultaneously hold titles of CEO, CTO, and Chairman

---

## Valuation & Share Price Indicators

| Metric | Value | Context |
|--------|-------|---------|
| Target IPO valuation | $1.25–$1.75 trillion (up to $2T) | IPO range |
| Target capital raise | ~$75 billion | Would be largest IPO in history |
| Series N preferred price | $270/share pre-split ($54 post-split) | Most recent preferred round |
| RSA grant-date fair value | $93.87/share (post-split) | As of Dec 31, 2025 |
| Implied equity value at RSA FV | ~$1.175 trillion | 12.5B shares × $93.87 |
| Late 2024 tender offers | $180–$210 billion | Secondary market |

---

## Corporate Structure

The IPO entity is a **combined company** with three segments following two mergers:
1. **Space segment** — Falcon 9, Falcon Heavy, Starship, Dragon
2. **Connectivity segment** — Starlink (~10.3M subscribers as of Q1 2026)
3. **AI segment** — xAI (Grok), X social media (~550M MAUs), COLOSSUS data centers (1 GW)

**Key mergers:**
- March 28, 2025: X Holdings Corp (Twitter/X) acquired by xAI
- February 2, 2026: xAI Holdings Corp acquired by SpaceX

**Balance Sheet (March 31, 2026):** Total assets $102.1B | Cash $15.9B | Total liabilities $60.5B | Shareholders' equity $34.5B | Backlog $28.4B

---

**Data vintage note:** Research data last updated May 24, 2026. Primary source is the S-1 filing on SEC EDGAR. For the most current information (e.g., final pricing, whether June 12 listing date held), direct verification is recommended.

key_facts: SpaceX filed a public S-1 registration statement with the SEC on May 20, 2026 (Accession Number: 0001628280-26-036936), following a confidential DRS filing on March 30, 2026.
Pro forma total shares outstanding are approximately 12.5 billion (12,521 million) as of March 31, 2026, reflecting a 5-for-1 stock split effective May 4, 2026.
SpaceX has a dual-class share structure: Class A (1 vote/share, public) and Class B (10 votes/share, held by Elon Musk and insiders).
Elon Musk controls approximately 85% of total shareholder voting power through Class B super-voting shares.
SpaceX plans to list on Nasdaq and Nasdaq Texas under ticker 'SPCX' with a target listing date of June 12, 2026.

Target IPO valuation ranges from $1.25 trillion to $1.75 trillion, with some estimates up to $2 trillion; the target capital raise is approximately $75 billion, which would make it the largest IPO in history.

Elon Musk holds a 1 billion restricted share award contingent on achieving a $7.5 trillion market cap and establishing a permanent Mars colony with 1 million inhabitants; these are NOT included in outstanding share counts.
The IPO entity is a combined company (SpaceX + xAI + X/Twitter) with three segments: Space, Connectivity (Starlink, 10.3M subscribers), and AI (Grok, X with ~550M MAUs).
Most recent RSA grant-date fair value was $93.87/share (post-split) as of December 31, 2025, implying ~$1.175 trillion equity value.
Underwriters include Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, BofA Securities, Citigroup, J.P. Morgan, Barclays, Deutsche Bank, RBC, UBS, Wells Fargo, and Allen & Company.
